audio_vae.tooltip: The Audio VAE model to use for encoding.
The Audio VAE model used for decoding the latent.

batch_size.tooltip: The number of latent audio samples in the batch.

audio_vae.tooltip: The Audio VAE model to get configuration from.

stretch.tooltip: Stretch the sigmas to be in the range [terminal, 1].

terminal.tooltip: The terminal value of the sigmas after stretching.

image.tooltip: Image or video to condition the latent video on. Must be 8*n + 1 frames. If the video is not 8*n + 1 frames, it will be cropped to the nearest 8*n + 1 frames.

frame_idx.tooltip: Frame index to start the conditioning at. For single-frame images or videos with 1-8 frames, any frame_idx value is acceptable. For videos with 9+ frames, frame_idx must be divisible by 8, otherwise it will be rounded down to the nearest multiple of 8. Negative values are counted from the end of the video.

img_compression.tooltip: Amount of compression to apply on image.

frame_idx.tooltip: Frame index to start the conditioning at. For single-frame images or videos with 1-8 frames, any frame_idx value is acceptable. For videos with 9+ frames, frame_idx must be divisible by 8, otherwise it will be rounded down to the nearest multiple of 8. Negative values are counted from the end of the video.

strength.tooltip: Strength of the conditioning. Higher values will make the conditioning more exact.

crf.tooltip: CRF value for the video. Higher values mean more motion, lower values mean higher quality.

blur_radius.tooltip: Blur kernel radius size. Higher values mean more motion, lower values mean higher quality.

LTXVAddGuideAdvanced.name: LTXVAddGuideAdvanced
LTXVAddGuideAdvanced.description: Adds a conditioning frame or a video at a specific frame index. This node is used to add a keyframe or a video segment which should appear in the generated video at a specified index. It resizes the image to the correct size and applies preprocessing to it.

frame_idx.tooltip: Frame index to start the conditioning at. Negative values are counted from the end of the video.

strength.tooltip: Strength of the conditioning. Higher values make it more exact.

crf.tooltip: CRF value. Higher = more motion, lower = higher quality.

blur_radius.tooltip: Blur kernel radius. Higher = more motion.

attention_strength.tooltip: Controls how strongly this guide influences generation via self-attention. 1.0 = full conditioning, 0.0 = ignore.

attention_mask.tooltip: Optional pixel-space spatial mask. Shape (F, H, W) or (H, W). Values in [0, 1]. Controls per-region conditioning influence. Multiplied by attention_strength.

LTXVAddGuideAdvancedAttention.name: LTXVAddGuideAdvancedAttention
LTXVAddGuideAdvancedAttention.description: Adds a conditioning frame/video at a specific frame index with per-guide attention strength control. Same preprocessing as LTXVAddGuideAdvanced, plus attention_strength and optional spatial attention_mask.

latent_idx.tooltip: Latent index to start the conditioning at. Can be negative toindicate that the conditioning is on the frames before the latent.

LTXVAddLatentGuide.name: LTXVAddLatentGuide
LTXVAddLatentGuide.description: Adds a keyframe or a video segment at a specific frame index.

LTXVImgToVideoConditionOnly.name: LTXVImgToVideoConditionOnly
LTXVImgToVideoConditionOnly.display_name: 🅛🅣🅧 LTXV Img To Video Condition Only
LTXVImgToVideoConditionOnly.description: Applies image conditioning to the first frames of an existing latent. Creates a noise mask to control conditioning strength.

factors.tooltip: Comma-separated list of factors, each factor will be used for one step.

model.tooltip: The model to apply the STG to.

block_indices.tooltip: Comma-separated indices of the blocks to apply the STG to.

LTXVApplySTG.name: LTXVApplySTG
LTXVApplySTG.description: Defines the blocks to apply the STG to.

guider.tooltip: The guider to use, must be a STGGuiderAdvanced.

noise.tooltip: The noise to use for the sampling.

optional_cond_images.tooltip: The images to use for conditioning the sampling.

optional_cond_indices.tooltip: The indices of the images to use for conditioning the sampling.

strength.tooltip: The strength of the conditioning on the images.

crop.tooltip: The crop mode to use for the images.

crf.tooltip: The CRF value to use for preprocessing the images.

blur.tooltip: The blur value to use for preprocessing the images.

guider.tooltip: The guider to use, must be a STGGuiderAdvanced.

noise.tooltip: The noise to use for the sampling.

guiding_latents.tooltip: The latents to use for guiding the sampling, typically with an IC-LoRA.

optional_cond_images.tooltip: The image to use for conditioning the sampling, if not provided, the sampling will be unconditioned (t2v setup). The image will be resized to the size of the first frame.

num_frames.tooltip: If -1, the number of frames will be based on the number of frames in the guiding_latents.

latents.tooltip: The latents of the video to extend.

num_new_frames.tooltip: If -1, the number of frames will be based on the number of frames in the optional_guiding_latents.

frame_overlap.tooltip: The overlap region to use for conditioning the new frames on the end of the provided latents.

guider.tooltip: The guider to use, must be a STGGuiderAdvanced.

noise.tooltip: The noise to use for the sampling.

strength.tooltip: The strength of the conditioning on the overlapping latents, when using optional_guiding_latents.

optional_guiding_latents.tooltip: Optional latents to guide the sampling.

optional_cond_images.tooltip: The images to use for conditioning the sampling.

cond_image_strength.tooltip: The strength of the conditioning on the images.

invert_input_masks.tooltip: Invert the input masks before processing.Useful for masking vs unmasking operations.

ignore_first_mask.tooltip: Zero out the first mask, typically used whenit corresponds to the conditioning frame.

pooling_method.tooltip: Method to combine temporal masks. Max preserves strongest values, mean averages them, min takes weakest values.

grow_mask.tooltip: Pixels to grow (positive) or shrink (negative) the mask.Uses morphological operations.

tapered_corners.tooltip: Use tapered corners in morphological operations for smoother mask edges.

clamp_min.tooltip: Minimum value to clamp masks to, ensuring minimum opacity.

clamp_max.tooltip: Maximum value to clamp masks to.

LTXVPreprocessMasks.name: LTXVPreprocessMasks
LTXVPreprocessMasks.description: Preprocess masks to be used for masking latents in the LTXVideo model.

LTXVPromptEnhancer.name: LTXVPromptEnhancer
LTXVPromptEnhancer.description: Enhances text prompts for image generation using LLMs. Optionally incorporates reference images to create more contextually relevant descriptions.

llm_name.tooltip: The hugging face name of the llm model to load.

image_captioner_name.tooltip: The hugging face name of the image captioning model to load.

LTXVPromptEnhancerLoader.name: LTXVPromptEnhancerLoader
LTXVPromptEnhancerLoader.description: Downloads and initializes LLM and image captioning models from Hugging Face to enhance text prompts for image generation.

LTXVSelectLatents.name: LTXVSelectLatents
LTXVSelectLatents.description: Selects a range of frames from the video latent. start_index and end_index define a closed interval (inclusive of both endpoints).

LTXVSetVideoLatentNoiseMasks.name: LTXVSetVideoLatentNoiseMasks
LTXVSetVideoLatentNoiseMasks.display_name: 🅛🅣🅧 LTXV Set Video Latent Noise Masks
LTXVSetVideoLatentNoiseMasks.description: Applies multiple masks to a video latent. masks can be 2D, 3D, or 4D tensors. If there are fewer masks than frames, the last mask will be reused.

guider.tooltip: The guider to use, must be a STGGuiderAdvanced.

latents.tooltip: The latents to use for creating the long video, they can be guiding latents or empty latents when no guidance is used.

temporal_tile_size.tooltip: The size of the temporal tile to use for the sampling, in pixel frames, in addition to the overlapping region.

temporal_overlap.tooltip: The overlap between the temporal tiles, in pixel frames.

guiding_strength.tooltip: The strength of the conditioning on guiding latents, when optional_guiding_latents are provided.

temporal_overlap_cond_strength.tooltip: The strength of the conditioning on the latents from the previous temporal tile.

cond_image_strength.tooltip: The strength of the conditioning on the optional_cond_images.

optional_cond_images.tooltip: The image to use for conditioning the first frame in the video (i2v setup). If not provided, the first frame will be unconditioned (t2v setup). The image will be resized to the size of the first frame.

optional_guiding_latents.tooltip: The latents to use for guiding the sampling, typically with an IC-LoRA.

adain_factor.tooltip: The strength of the AdaIn operation used to fix the statistics of each new generated temporal tile, to prevent accumulated oversaturation.

optional_positive_conditionings.tooltip: Optional way to provide changing positive prompts, one per temporal tile, using the MultiPromptProvider node.

optional_negative_index_latents.tooltip: Special optional latents to condition on a negative index before each new temporal tile as a way to provide long term context during video generation.

guiding_start_step.tooltip: The step at which the guiding latents start to be used.

guiding_end_step.tooltip: The step at which the guiding latents stop to be used.

optional_cond_image_indices.tooltip: The indices of the keyframes to use for the sampling, separated by commas. The indices are counted from the end of the video.

optional_normalizing_latents.tooltip: The latents to use for normalizing the output latents, they will be used to normalize the output latents to the same statistics as the input latents.

prompts.tooltip: Prompts to encode, one per line. Each prompt will be encoded separately. Each prompt will be used in one temporal_tile in LTXVLoopingSampler.

clip.tooltip: CLIP model to encode the prompts.

percentile.tooltip: Percentile of distribution to use for statistics calculation

factors.tooltip: Comma-separated list of factors, each factor will be used for one step.

percentile.tooltip: Percentile of distribution to use for statistics calculation

LTXVPerStepStatNormPatcher.name: LTXVPerStepStatNormPatcher
LTXVPerStepStatNormPatcher.display_name: 🅛🅣🅧 LTXV Per Step Stat Norm Patcher

gemma_path.tooltip: The name of the text encoder model to load.

ltxv_path.tooltip: The name of the ltxv model to load.

system_prompt.default:
You are a Creative Assistant. Given a user's raw input prompt describing a scene or concept, expand it into a detailed video generation prompt with specific visuals and integrated audio to guide a text-to-video model.

#### Guidelines
- Strictly follow all aspects of the user's raw input: include every element requested (style, visuals, motions, actions, camera movement, audio).
    - If the input is vague, invent concrete details: lighting, textures, materials, scene settings, etc.
        - For characters: describe gender, clothing, hair, expressions. DO NOT invent unrequested characters.
- Use active language: present-progressive verbs ("is walking," "speaking"). If no action specified, describe natural movements.
- Maintain chronological flow: use temporal connectors ("as," "then," "while").
- Audio layer: Describe complete soundscape (background audio, ambient sounds, SFX, speech/music when requested). Integrate sounds chronologically alongside actions. Be specific (e.g., "soft footsteps on tile"), not vague (e.g., "ambient sound is present").
- Speech (only when requested):
    - For ANY speech-related input (talking, conversation, singing, etc.), ALWAYS include exact words in quotes with voice characteristics (e.g., "The man says in an excited voice: 'You won't believe what I just saw!'").
    - Specify language if not English and accent if relevant.
- Style: Include visual style at the beginning: "Style: <style>, <rest of prompt>." Default to cinematic-realistic if unspecified. Omit if unclear.
- Visual and audio only: NO non-visual/auditory senses (smell, taste, touch).
- Restrained language: Avoid dramatic/exaggerated terms. Use mild, natural phrasing.
    - Colors: Use plain terms ("red dress"), not intensified ("vibrant blue," "bright red").
    - Lighting: Use neutral descriptions ("soft overhead light"), not harsh ("blinding light").
    - Facial features: Use delicate modifiers for subtle features (i.e., "subtle freckles").

#### Important notes:
- Analyze the user's raw input carefully. In cases of FPV or POV, exclude the description of the subject whose POV is requested.
- Camera motion: DO NOT invent camera motion unless requested by the user.
- Speech: DO NOT modify user-provided character dialogue unless it's a typo.
- No timestamps or cuts: DO NOT use timestamps or describe scene cuts unless explicitly requested.
- Format: DO NOT use phrases like "The scene opens with...". Start directly with Style (optional) and chronological scene description.
- Format: DO NOT start your response with special characters.
- DO NOT invent dialogue unless the user mentions speech/talking/singing/conversation.
- If the user's raw input prompt is highly detailed, chronological and in the requested format: DO NOT make major edits or introduce new elements. Add/enhance audio descriptions if missing.

#### Output Format (Strict):
- Single continuous paragraph in natural language (English).
- NO titles, headings, prefaces, code fences, or Markdown.
- If unsafe/invalid, return original user prompt. Never ask questions or clarifications.

Your output quality is CRITICAL. Generate visually rich, dynamic prompts with integrated audio for high-quality video generation.

#### Example
Input: "A woman at a coffee shop talking on the phone"
Output:
Style: realistic with cinematic lighting. In a medium close-up, a woman in her early 30s with shoulder-length brown hair sits at a small wooden table by the window. She wears a cream-colored turtleneck sweater, holding a white ceramic coffee cup in one hand and a smartphone to her ear with the other. Ambient cafe sounds fill the space—espresso machine hiss, quiet conversations, gentle clinking of cups. The woman listens intently, nodding slightly, then takes a sip of her coffee and sets it down with a soft clink. Her face brightens into a warm smile as she speaks in a clear, friendly voice, 'That sounds perfect! I'd love to meet up this weekend. How about Saturday afternoon?' She laughs softly—a genuine chuckle—and shifts in her chair. Behind her, other patrons move subtly in and out of focus. 'Great, I'll see you then,' she concludes cheerfully, lowering the phone.

LTXVGemmaEnhancePrompt.name: LTXVGemmaEnhancePrompt
LTXVGemmaEnhancePrompt.description: Enhance text prompts using Gemma 3 VLLM for improved video generation.

latent.tooltip: Video-only latent to condition. Must be a 5D video latent (batch, channels, frames, height, width).

frame_idx.tooltip: Frame index to start the conditioning at. For single-frame videos, any frame_idx value is acceptable. For videos, frame_idx must be 1 modulo 8, otherwise it will be rounded down to the nearest 1 modulo 8. Negative values are counted from the end of the video.

latent_downscale_factor.tooltip: For IC-LoRA on small grid. 1 means original size, 2 means half size, 3 means third, etc.

crop.tooltip: Crop mode when resizing. 'center' crops to fit, 'disabled' stretches to fit.

use_tiled_encode.tooltip: Enable tiled VAE encoding for large resolutions/long videos to reduce memory usage.

tile_size.tooltip: Spatial tile size for tiled encoding. Only used when use_tiled_encode is enabled.

tile_overlap.tooltip: Overlap between tiles for tiled encoding. Only used when use_tiled_encode is enabled.

LTXAddVideoICLoRAGuide.name: LTXAddVideoICLoRAGuide
LTXAddVideoICLoRAGuide.description: Adds one or more conditioning frames starting at the specified frame index. Supports both single images and multi-frame videos. The latent_downscale_factor resizes input to a fraction of the target size (1 = original, 2 = half, 3 = third, etc.) for IC-LoRA on small grids.

latent.tooltip: Video-only latent to condition. Must be a 5D video latent.

frame_idx.tooltip: Frame index to start the conditioning at. The value is rounded to the nearest frame and wrapped modulo the number of video frames. Negative values are counted from the end of the video before wrapping.

latent_downscale_factor.tooltip: For IC-LoRA on small grid. 1 = original size, 2 = half, etc.

attention_strength.tooltip: Controls how strongly this guide influences generation via self-attention. 1.0 = full conditioning (default), 0.0 = ignore. When an attention_mask is also provided, this multiplies the mask values.

attention_mask.tooltip: Optional pixel-space spatial mask. Shape (F, H, W) or (H, W). Values in [0, 1]. Controls per-region conditioning influence. Multiplied by attention_strength.

LTXAddVideoICLoRAGuideAdvanced.name: LTXAddVideoICLoRAGuideAdvanced
LTXAddVideoICLoRAGuideAdvanced.description: Adds IC-LoRA guide conditioning with per-guide attention strength control. Same as LTXAddVideoICLoRAGuide, but allows controlling how strongly this guide influences generation via self-attention, optionally with a spatial mask.

LTXICLoRALoaderModelOnly.name: LTXICLoRALoaderModelOnly
LTXICLoRALoaderModelOnly.description: Loads a LoRA model and extracts the latent_downscale_factor from the safetensors metadata.

tracks.tooltip: JSON string of track coordinates (list of point lists with x/y keys).

LTXVDrawTracks.name: LTXVDrawTracks
LTXVDrawTracks.description: GPU-accelerated sparse track renderer. Rasterises circles at high resolution and downscales with bilinear interpolation.

image.tooltip: Reference image displayed as the editor canvas background.

points_store.tooltip: JSON array of spline control points managed by the editor widget.

coordinates.tooltip: JSON array of interpolated track coordinates produced by the editor.

points_to_sample.tooltip: Number of points sampled along each spline curve.

LTXVSparseTrackEditor.name: LTXVSparseTrackEditor
LTXVSparseTrackEditor.description: Interactive spline editor for drawing sparse motion tracks on a reference image.

spatial_radius.tooltip: Half-size of the spatial dilation kernel. Kernel = 2*radius+1.

temporal_radius.tooltip: Half-size of the temporal dilation kernel. Kernel = 2*radius+1.

mask.tooltip: Video mask to dilate. Either this or image_as_mask must be provided.

image_as_mask.tooltip: Image to use as mask (channel-averaged). Either this or mask must be provided.

LTXVDilateVideoMask.name: LTXVDilateVideoMask
LTXVDilateVideoMask.description: Dilates a video mask spatially and/or temporally using separable max-pooling and thresholds the result.

images.tooltip: Video frames to composite onto the green background.

mask.tooltip: Mask indicating regions to replace with green. Single-frame masks are broadcast.

LTXVInpaintPreprocess.name: LTXVInpaintPreprocess
LTXVInpaintPreprocess.description: Composites images with a green background where mask is active, for inpainting conditioning.

crf.tooltip: CRF value for the video. Higher values mean more motion, lower values mean higher quality.

blur_radius.tooltip: Blur kernel radius size. Higher values mean more motion, lower values mean higher quality.

LTXVImgToVideoAdvanced.name: LTXVImgToVideoAdvanced
LTXVImgToVideoAdvanced.description: Adds a conditioning frame or a video at index 0. This node is used to add a keyframe or a video segment which should appear in the generated video at index 0. It resizes the image to the correct size and applies preprocessing to it.

LTXVAddLatents.name: LTXVAddLatents
LTXVAddLatents.description: Concatenates two video latents along the frames dimension. latents1 and latents2 must have the same dimensions except for the frames dimension.

LTXVDilateLatent.name: LTXVDilateLatent
LTXVDilateLatent.description: Dilates a latent by a grid size.

LTXVSetAudioVideoMaskByTime.name: LTXVSetAudioVideoMaskByTime
LTXVSetAudioVideoMaskByTime.display_name: 🅛🅣🅧 LTXV Set Audio Video Mask By Time
LTXVSetAudioVideoMaskByTime.description: Sets the audio and video mask by time.

spatial_tiles.tooltip: The number of spatial tiles to use, horizontal and vertical.

spatial_overlap.tooltip: The overlap between the spatial tiles. (in latent frames)

temporal_tile_length.tooltip: The length of the temporal tile to use for the sampling, in latent frames, including the overlapping region.

temporal_overlap.tooltip: The overlap between the temporal tiles, in latent frames.

last_frame_fix.tooltip: If true, the last frame will be repeated and discarded after the decoding.

working_device.tooltip: The device to use for the decoding. auto->same as the latents.

working_dtype.tooltip: The data type to use for the decoding. auto->same as the latents.

LTXVSpatioTemporalTiledVAEDecode.name: LTXVSpatioTemporalTiledVAEDecode
LTXVSpatioTemporalTiledVAEDecode.display_name: 🅛🅣🅧 LTXV Spatio Temporal Tiled VAE Decode
